title: Dave vs. OppFi: Which Fintech Stock Is the Smarter Bet Right Now?
text:
Dave operates as a neobank offering small, interest-free cash advances up to $500 through its ExtraCash service, plus a $5 monthly subscription. The company emphasizes financial management and income opportunity services.

OppFi focuses on serving underbanked and subprime customers through an alternative lending platform powered by artificial intelligence and machine learning technology.

Growth Performance:
Dave demonstrates stronger momentum with 49% year-over-year growth in ExtraCash originations and 63% year-over-year top-line growth as of Q3 2025. The company raised full-year revenue guidance to $544-$547 million.

OppFi shows more moderate expansion, registering 13.5% year-over-year increase in its top line with adjusted net income climbing 41.4% in the same period. The company also raised 2025 guidance multiple times throughout the year.

Credit Risk Management:
Dave deployed CashAI v5.5 to reduce delinquency rates, achieving a 28-day delinquency rate of 2.33% by Q3 2025. OppFi's Model 6 reduced net charge-offs as a percentage of revenues by 430 basis points year-over-year, demonstrating strong loan quality improvements.

Valuation:
The critical distinction emerges in pricing: OppFi trades at a forward P/E of 6.47X versus Dave's 15.81X. The analysis concludes OppFi represents the better value proposition despite Dave's superior growth trajectory.
